title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
code: crukButton(inputId, text, type = "primary", icon = NULL, ...)

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
text: inputId input slot used access value. text button label text display. type Character string specifying button style. Either "primary" (default, magenta background) "secondary" (white background magenta border). icon Optional character string specifying Google Material Symbol display. Must one : "upload_file", "download", "check_circle", "open_in_new", "content_copy", "mail", "share". Default NULL (icon). ... Additional arguments passed shiny::actionButton().

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
text: Shiny action button tag CRUK branding attached CSS/icon   dependencies.

title: Create a CRUK-branded action button — crukButton
code: if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)    ui <- fluidPage(     crukButton("btn1", "Primary Button", type = "primary"),     crukButton("btn2", "Download", type = "secondary", icon = "download"),     crukButton("btn3", "Share", icon = "share")   )    server <- function(input, output, session) {     observeEvent(input$btn1, {       print("Button clicked!")     })   }    shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Border-less Card for Chart and Table Combined Output — crukChartTable

title: Round Numbers Following Cancer Intelligence Guidelines — crukRounding
text: function applies different rounding rules based number magnitude: Numbers >= 100,000: Rounded 3 significant figures qualifiers Numbers >= 1,000: Rounded nearest 100 "around" prefix Numbers >= 100: Rounded nearest 10 "around" prefix Numbers >= 10: Rounded nearest 5 "around" prefix Numbers < 10: Simplified "around 10", "around 5", "less 5" Special handling applied avoid ties 0.5 boundaries adding small increments rounding.

title: Get Formatted Sources from Zotero Collection — get_zotero_sources
text: collection_path Character string. Path Zotero collection using forward slashes separate nested collections (e.g., "General & Cross Pathway/Local Stats Dashboard". function navigates collection hierarchy find target collection. group_id Numeric. Zotero group library ID. Default 6230705 (CRUK team library). Use find_zotero_group find group ID using different library. api_key Character string. Zotero API key authentication. Default reads ZOTERO_API_KEY environment variable. Get API key https://www.zotero.org/settings/keys last_accessed Character string NULL. Optional custom "Last accessed" text override access date stored Zotero. Useful standardising access dates across multiple sources. Format: "Last accessed Month, Year" (e.g., "Last accessed January, 2024"). NULL (default), uses access date Zotero item's metadata.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: Track browser window dimensions Shiny app using Shiny module. module provides UI server components work together monitor window size changes.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
code: window_size_ui(id)  window_size_server(id, debounce_ms = 200, verbose = FALSE)

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: id Character string. Module namespace ID used uniquely identify instance module. Must UI server functions. debounce_ms Numeric. Debounce delay milliseconds (default: 200). prevents excessive reactive updates rapid window resizing. verbose Logical. `TRUE`, prints window size changes R console timestamps (default: `FALSE`). Useful debugging.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: **window_size_ui**: `shiny.tag` object containing JavaScript code. **window_size_server**: debounced reactive expression returning list :   * `width` - Numeric. Window inner width pixels   * `height` - Numeric. Window inner height pixels

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: JavaScript code sends initial window dimensions Shiny connection established listens window resize events. random nonce included resize ensure Shiny detects changes even resizing back previous dimensions. returned reactive `NULL` contain `NA` values first window size update received. Always use [shiny::req()] similar validation accessing values. debouncing helps performance limiting frequently app responds window resize events. 200ms delay generally imperceptible users significantly reducing server load.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: `window_size_ui(id)` creates JavaScript handlers track browser window dimensions. Adds client-side code sends window size information server initial load whenever window resized.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
text: `window_size_server(id, debounce_ms = 200, verbose = FALSE)` server-side component returns debounced reactive expression containing current window width height.

title: Window Size Tracking Module — window_size
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) library(ggplot2)  ui <- fluidPage(   window_size_ui("win_tracker"),   verbatimTextOutput("size_display"),   plotOutput("responsive_plot") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Track window size with verbose logging   win_size <- window_size_server("win_tracker",     debounce_ms = 300,     verbose = TRUE   )    # Display current size   output$size_display <- renderText({     ws <- win_size()     req(ws)     paste0("Width: ", ws$width, "px\nHeight: ", ws$height, "px")   })    # Create responsive plot based on window size   output$responsive_plot <- renderPlot({     ws <- win_size()     req(ws)      # Adjust plot based on window width     point_size <- if (ws$width < 768) 2 else 3      ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +       geom_point(size = point_size) +       theme_minimal()   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) } # }
